zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Rudd於1422年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾易斯特時，她的父親Vilhelm Mikkelsen Rudd44歲，母親Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstand44歲。她和Henry Freeman至少有2個兒子。她去世於1486年在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾易斯特享年64歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾易爾林波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Rudd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for someone with a ruddy complexion, from Middle English rudde ‘redness, ruddiness, rosy hue’ (Old English rudu).
English: habitational name from Rhude in Holsworthy (Devon), apparently named from Middle English rude (Old English hrēod) ‘reed bed’. Compare Rhode 2.
American shortened form of any of various Jewish surnames beginning with Rud-.

variants.name: Rudkin
meanings: nickname from Middle English rud(de) ‘red’ + the diminutive suffix -kin. Compare Rudd .
from a Middle English pet form of the Old Norse female personal name Rudda, of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Ruddell
meanings: English: habitational name from Ruddle in Newnham (Gloucestershire) or Rudhall in Ross (Herefordshire). The Gloucestershire placename may derive from Old English hrēod ‘reed’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’, though an error due to confusion with the Gloucestershire placename Rodley may mean that the second element comes from Old English hyll ‘hill’. The Herefordshire placename derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ with an uncertain first element, perhaps the Old English plant-name rūde ‘rue’ or Middle English rud ‘marigold’.
English: nickname from a diminutive of Middle English rud(de) ‘red’. Compare Rudd .
English and Irish (Armagh): variant of   Riddell  .

variants.name: Ruddy
meanings: English: nickname for a person with a ruddy complexion, from Middle English rudi ‘ruddy’ (see Rudd 1).
English: habitational name from a lost or as yet unidentified place, perhaps in the West Midlands.

variants.name: Rhode
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English rode (Old English rod, rodu) designating someone who lived by a woodland clearing, or for someone who came from a place so named, such as Road (Somerset), Rhode (Somerset), Road Farm in Colebrook (Devon), Rhode in Halberton (Devon), Roade (Northamptonshire), Rode (Cheshire), and Rodd (Herefordshire).
English: topographic name for someone who lived by a reed bed, from Middle English rode (Old English hrēod). See also Rude and Trude .
German: habitational name from any places called Rhode (Lower Saxony, Rhineland) and Rhoden (Hesse, Saxony-Anhalt, former Silesia).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Rudd was born in 1422, in Irchester, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Vilhelm Mikkelsen Rudd, was 44 and her mother, Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstand, was 44. She had at least 2 sons with Henry Freeman. She died in 1486, in Irchester, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Irthlingborough, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Rudd est née en 1422, à Irchester, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Vilhelm Mikkelsen Rudd, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstand, avait 44 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Henry Freeman. Elle est décédée en 1486, à Irchester, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Irthlingborough, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Rudd 1422 in Irchester, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Vilhelm Mikkelsen Rudd 44 und ihre Mutter Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstand 44. Sie hatte mit Henry Freeman mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1486 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Irchester, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Irthlingborough, Northamptonshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Rudd è nata il nel 1422 nel Irchester, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Vilhelm Mikkelsen Rudd, aveva 44 anni e sua madre, Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstand, aveva 44 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Henry Freeman. Morì nel 1486, nel Irchester, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Irthlingborough, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Ruddが1422年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアイルチェスターで生まれた時，父Vilhelm Mikkelsen Ruddは44歳，母Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstandは44歳であった。彼女はHenry Freemanとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1486年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアイルチェスターで64歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアイルスリングボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Rudd(이)가 1422년 영국 노르섬프토실르 이르체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Vilhelm Mikkelsen Rudd(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstand(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 Henry Freeman(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1486년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 이르체스터에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 이르슬링버루그h에 묻혔다.

Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Henry Freeman. Ela faleceu em 1486, em Irchester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Irthlingborough, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Rudd родилась в 1422 г. в местности ирчестир, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Vilhelm Mikkelsen Rudd было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstand, 44. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Henry Freeman. Она умерла в 1486 г. в местности ирчестир, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности иртлингбороую, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Rudd nació en 1422, en Irchester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Vilhelm Mikkelsen Rudd, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Helle Henriksdatter Ulfstand, tenía 44 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Henry Freeman. Murió en 1486, en Irchester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Irthlingborough, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
